meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for student developers.
content.answer_block: Stop manually copying folders. By implementing Claude Code with a version control workflow, you automate repository initialization and commit history. This setup ensures every change is tracked, recoverable, and professional. You will move from 'folder-based' chaos to a streamlined Git-managed environment that demonstrates high-level engineering habits to future employers.
content.intro:
You’re staring at 'project_final_v2_real.zip' on your desktop, heart racing, knowing that one wrong keystroke will delete hours of work. You’ve bypassed version control because it felt like a hurdle, but now it’s the glass ceiling preventing you from building real-world software. This is the moment where amateur coding ends and professional engineering begins.

We are bridging that gap. By integrating Claude Code with modern automation tools, we’re turning your local environment into an industrial-grade development workspace. You aren’t just fixing a workflow issue; you’re adopting the same architectural mindset used by senior engineers at top-tier SaaS companies.

steps.title: Initialize the Local Repository
steps.description: Before automating, we must establish a Git foundation to track our changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standard .gitignore file for a Node.js project and initialize a local git repository.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Grant Claude Code the necessary permissions to interface with your file system and Git history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Claude Code configuration that allows recursive file reading and automated commit messaging.

steps.title: Automate Commit Documentation
steps.description: Use AI to generate meaningful commit messages, transforming your history into a professional portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current diff and write a concise, conventional-commit style message for the changes.

steps.title: Establish Branching Strategy
steps.description: Learn to isolate features so your main code remains stable while you experiment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Help me create a new feature branch named 'feature/auth-setup' and switch to it.

steps.title: Verify History Integrity
steps.description: Audit your repository to ensure your AI-assisted work is properly logged and recoverable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the last 5 commits and verify there are no missing files or uncommitted changes.

pro_tips: Always use 'Conventional Commits' (feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your Git history readable for recruiters.
Never commit API keys; use .env files and add them to your .gitignore immediately.
Use Claude Code to write your README.md files; it understands the project architecture better than you do.
Keep your branches short-lived; merge back to main every 24 hours to avoid 'merge hell'.
Treat your Git history as a narrative of your problem-solving skills, not just a backup tool.
content.next_steps: Once your local environment is stable, push your repository to GitHub or GitLab and begin deploying to a live hosting platform like Vercel or Railway.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation that separates hobbyists from professional developers. Don't stop here—deploy your first project and let the world see what you've built. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
